title: No documentation on when to use LICHEN_L2_MTU vs LICHEN_LORA_MTU
description:
Locations:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.h:49 - LICHEN_L2_MTU = 200
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.h:55-57 - LICHEN_LORA_MTU = 200

Risk: Two identical MTU constants exist in separate headers with different names. A BUILD_ASSERT at lichen_l2.c:33-34 verifies they match, but:

1. A contributor adding new code may not know which constant to use
2. The header comments explain the purpose differently (L2_MTU focuses on IPv6, LORA_MTU focuses on framing overhead)
3. If a contributor uses LICHEN_LORA_MTU in L2 code or vice versa, it works but creates confusion

Pattern contributors will misuse: Using the wrong constant because it works but does not match semantic intent.

Suggested fix: Either:
1. Document clearly in both headers when to use which (L2_MTU for application-facing code, LORA_MTU for driver-facing code)
2. Or unify to a single constant in a shared header that both files include
